name: NEET UG
purpose: Mandatory entrance exam for admission to MBBS/BDS courses in India.
typicalWindow: Applications usually open ~Dec–Feb; exam typically held ~May.
eligibility: Passed 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology and English; minimum 50% aggregate (40% for reserved categories); minimum age 17 by Dec 31 of the admission year.

name: NEET PG
conductingBody: NBEMS (National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences)
purpose: Entrance exam for admission to MD/MS and PG Diploma courses after MBBS.
eligibility: MBBS degree from a recognized institution and completion of the compulsory rotating internship.

name: NExT
purpose: Proposed licentiate exam set to replace NEET PG and FMGE — a final MBBS/licensing exam and the gateway to postgraduate medical admission.
typicalWindow: Expected for batches graduating 2028–29 or later; windows to be announced by the conducting body.
eligibility: All MBBS students from NMC-approved colleges.
